architecture.summary: DMoE is a modular knowledge injection system that decouples lightweight expert modules (LoRA adapters) and a BM25-based router from a frozen base LLM. During autoregressive generation, an uncertainty-aware router monitors token entropy (TU) and selectively activates relevant experts via BM25 retrieval over expert text surrogates when the model's confidence drops below a threshold. Experts are composed only into the final-layer feed-forward network to preserve KV-cache reuse during decoding. Information flows from the frozen base model's hidden states into triggered experts, whose outputs are composed back into the model's parameter space for the current decoding step.

biblio.title: Decoupled Mixture-of-Experts for Parametric Knowledge Injection

claims.statement: DMoE obtains the best or tied-best score on 11 out of 14 reported effectiveness metrics across knowledge-intensive benchmarks
KV-cache reuse consistently reduces latency by 3–4× and GPU memory usage by 2–3× across all datasets
DMoE reduces average inference latency from 20.0 seconds (OLMoE coupled MoE baseline) to 2.7–3.9 seconds
DMoE reduces GPU memory usage from 26.1 GB (OLMoE coupled MoE baseline) to 7.2–8.3 GB
Attaching experts only to the final-layer FFN guarantees that all cached keys and values remain valid, enabling full KV-cache compatibility during autoregressive decoding
DMoE uses a BM25-based lexical router implemented via an external inverted index, which is training-free, incrementally updatable, and avoids re-coupling routing with model parameters

Each expert is trained as a LoRA adapter with rank 4 on a single knowledge unit augmented with paraphrases and question-answer pairs, without using answer strings or gold supporting-document annotations
On HotpotQA with Qwen2.5-1.5B, Basic-RAG marginally outperforms DMoE on both EM and F1 metrics
